title: Lumen вҖ” a from-scratch path tracer
description: A physically based path tracer built from scratch in TypeScript. NEWEST (20.0) вҖ” SPHERE-LIGHT NEXT-EVENT ESTIMATION (subtended-cone sampling): for nineteen versions Lumen's NEE could connect a shadow ray only to TRIANGLE emitters вҖ” a flat triangle has an exact solid-angle pdf dВІ/(cosОёВ·A), so its MIS weights are provably consistent, but an emissive SPHERE has no such pdf and was left to BSDF sampling alone (a scattered ray that happens to strike it), which finds a small bright orb a fraction of a percent of the time, so a sphere-lit room rendered as a STORM OF FIREFLIES вҖ” the very premise of the Glowing Orb scene. 20.0 closes that gap with the textbook estimator (PBRT 'Sampling Spheres'): the directions from a shade point that hit a sphere of radius R a distance d away form a CONE of half-angle Оёmax with cosОёmax=вҲҡ(1вҲ’RВІ/dВІ), and sampling UNIFORMLY inside that cone gives a constant solid-angle density p(Пү)=1/О© with О©=2ПҖ(1вҲ’cosОёmax) вҖ” so every shadow ray lands ON the orb and none is wasted on the dark around it, with the near-hit distance computed in CLOSED FORM (dВ·cosОёвҲ’вҲҡ(RВІвҲ’dВІsinВІОё), robust at the grazing cone edge). The SAME О© drives the MIS weight when a BSDF ray instead lands on the sphere (sphereDirPdf), so the two estimators stay consistent and the result is PROVABLY UNBIASED вҖ” only the variance collapses. It is a drop-in for the SELECTION STEP ONLY (mirroring the 14.0 light tree): sampleLight/lightPdf/envSunPdf learn a useSphere flag, the emissive spheres join the uniform 1/N selection pool as a residual slot beside the light tree's triangle mass and the env sun, and the integrator is otherwise untouched вҖ” so the surface NEE, the VOLUMETRIC in-scattering NEE (a medium can now connect to a sphere), and the path tracer / path-guided / Metropolis estimators all inherit the win. It is gated behind a 'Sphere lights (cone NEE)' toggle (default off, on for two new scenes), and with it off every code path is the historical one BIT-FOR-BIT, so all 98 prior proofs stay green; a shade point INSIDE a sphere has no subtending cone, so the sampler declines there and BSDF sampling carries it unbiasedly. Two showcase scenes вҖ” Plasma Lamps (a still life lit only by three vivid emissive bulbs of varied size/distance, with sharp coloured reflections in a polished steel sphere) and Firefly Swarm (~50 tiny bright motes over a meadow, the sphere-light analogue of Star Field). Six new proofs (104 total): the subtended-cone solid angle equals 2ПҖ(1вҲ’cosОёmax) and REDUCES to the inverse-square ПҖRВІ/dВІ as R/dвҶ’0 (the point-light limit); the cone sampler MATCHES its pdf (вҲ«_cone dПү=О© by Monte-Carlo, every sampled ray actually intersects the sphere, pdfвүЎ1/О©); the directional pdf INTEGRATES TO 1 over the sphere of directions (a genuine probability distribution вҮ’ unbiased); the headline вҖ” the cone-NEE estimator matches the ANALYTIC SPHERE FORM FACTOR ПҒВ·LВ·sinВІОёmaxВ·cosОёc to under a standard error while its per-sample VARIANCE is over 100,000Г— below the naive hemisphere sampler (the firefly-storm regime removed); MIS CONSISTENCY (the sampler's pdf equals scene.lightPdf for the same direction to machine Оө, the no-double-count guarantee that keeps the BSDF-hit MIS weight exact); and the estimator respects the HORIZON (a sub-floor sphere leaks nothing) and DECLINES INSIDE a sphere. Also (19.0) вҖ” AgX TONE MAPPING: the final step вҖ” squeezing the unbounded HDR buffer into a monitor's 0вҖ“1 вҖ” is a real image-formation choice, and Lumen's old operators (ACES, Reinhard, Hejl filmic) were all PER-CHANNEL, which has a notorious failure: a bright saturated colour drives one channel to clip while the others lag, so the hue SKEWS (an intense blue light turns magenta, a fire's core goes pure red). 19.0 adds AgX (Troy Sobotka's transform, now the Blender default), which couples the channels so highlights DESATURATE TOWARD WHITE as they brighten, the way film and the eye do: it rotates linear colour through a desaturated inset matrix, compresses luminance over a fixed logвӮӮ window, applies a sigmoidal contrast curve (Wrensch's 6th-order fit), then rotates back through the outset matrix. It is purely a DISPLAY transform вҖ” it runs over the averaged buffer and never touches the unbiased light transport, so it cannot perturb any of the 94 existing proofs вҖ” selectable beside ACES/Filmic/Reinhard/Linear. Four new proofs (98 total): the contrast curve is monotone and bounded; a NEUTRAL GREY STAYS NEUTRAL (the row-stochastic inset/outset preserve the white point вҖ” the guard that caught the column-major matrix transpose, with 18% grey landing on display byte 128); blackвҶ’black with luminance monotone in exposure; and a blinding saturated colour provably desaturates toward white. Also (18.0) вҖ” PHYSICALLY BASED LIGHT COLOUR (BLACKBODY EMITTERS): a real light source has a TEMPERATURE, not an RGB colour вҖ” a tungsten lamp is ~2700 K and warm, daylight ~6500 K and neutral, a clear north sky ~10000 K and blue, the warmвҶ’cool sweep every photographer knows as colour temperature. That progression is the PLANCKIAN LOCUS, fixed by physics, and Lumen now computes it from scratch: planck(О»,T) is Planck's law for spectral radiance, integrated against the CIE 1931 COLOUR-MATCHING FUNCTIONS (the analytic WymanвҖ“SloanвҖ“Shirley fit, no 1 nm table) to get the tristimulus the eye sees, then converted XYZвҶ’linear-sRGB вҖ” so a scene specifies a light the way a real one is rated, blackbody(3200), and gets the physically correct hue. It needs no integrator or material change (a colour helper computed at scene-build time) yet is as grounded as the transport it feeds. New Colour Temperature scene вҖ” a row of panels from 2000 K to 12000 K washing neutral spheres, the locus read straight off physically-coloured light. Four new proofs (94 total): Planck positivity + WIEN'S DISPLACEMENT (numerically located peak О»_maxВ·T вүҲ 2.898Г—10вҒ¶ nmВ·K to <1%); STEFANвҖ“BOLTZMANN (band integral вҲқ TвҒҙ вҮ’ вҲ«B(2T)/вҲ«B(T) вүҲ 16); the locus runs warmвҶ’neutralвҶ’cool with a strictly monotone red/blue ratio, every hue bounded in [0,1]; and 6500 K lands on a near-neutral white point (the calibration anchor of the whole PlanckвҶ’CMFвҶ’XYZвҶ’sRGB pipeline). Also (17.0) вҖ” A SHARPER LIGHT TREE (SAH SPLITTING + RECEIVER-AWARE IMPORTANCE): two refinements to the 14.0 light BVH that the many-lights paper (Conty Estevez & Kulla 2018) builds in. First, the tree is now built with a SURFACE-AREA HEURISTIC вҖ” instead of splitting each cluster of emitters at the median, it minimises ОЈ powerВ·surfaceArea over every candidate split (a prefix/suffix sweep in O(count)), the light-transport analogue of the geometry BVH's SAH, so a lone bright lamp is isolated from a halo of dim ones and each descent's importance estimate is sharper. Second, the cluster importance is now RECEIVER-AWARE: it folds the shade point's SURFACE NORMAL into the weight (a floored cone bound on how much of the cluster's box lies in the lit hemisphere), so a cluster tucked BEHIND the surface вҖ” which the cosine term will zero out anyway вҖ” is heavily down-weighted instead of drawing half the shadow rays. Both stay UNBIASED (the selection pdf is still renormalised at every split, and a floor keeps every light strictly positive), threaded through sampleLight/lightPdf and kept MIS-consistent via the stored vertex normal, so the image is unchanged and only the noise drops; a receiver normal is optional everywhere, so volume/subsurface scatter vertices and every prior proof use the receiver-agnostic path verbatim. New Light Cage scene вҖ” a faceted object inside a full sphere of ~220 inward-facing lights, half of them behind any facet's normal, the regime where the receiver term wins most. Four new proofs (90 total): the receiver-aware pdf still sums to 1 for any point AND normal; its sampler matches its pdf; the receiver term provably steers selection mass to the lit hemisphere (a front cluster beats a coincident, equally-oriented back one) while staying normalised and positive; and on a sphere of lights half behind the receiver the receiver-aware and agnostic trees agree in the MEAN (unbiased) while the receiver-aware VARIANCE is clearly lower. Also (16.0) вҖ” CHROMATIC PARTICIPATING MEDIA (WHY THE SKY IS BLUE): a real atmosphere does not extinguish every colour equally вҖ” Rayleigh scattering strips BLUE from a beam far faster than red (Пғ_t вҲқ ~1/О»вҒҙ), which is exactly why the sky is blue and the setting sun is red. Lumen's media carried a SCALAR extinction (one Пғ_t for every colour), so a haze or cloud could only be tinted by what it SCATTERED, never by HOW FAR EACH COLOUR REACHED. 16.0 adds a CHROMATIC EXTINCTION (a per-wavelength Пғ_t on the medium) traced with the same hero-wavelength trick the rest of the renderer uses: a path entering the medium commits to one wavelength О», takes its RGB weight ONCE (E_О»[w]=(1,1,1), so the estimator stays UNBIASED), then DELTA-TRACKS the free-flight and RATIO-TRACKS the shadow transmittance against THAT wavelength's Пғ_t(О») вҖ” the colour reconstructing over many paths. Because both null-collision estimators only ever needed a scalar Пғ_t and a constant majorant, it is a one-line Пғ_tвҶ’Пғ_t(О») substitution that works for BOTH homogeneous (analytic) and heterogeneous (fBm cloud/smoke) media; the heterogeneous density shape is untouched, only the extinction SCALE is chromatic. The scalar path is preserved bit-for-bit so all 81 prior proofs stay green. Two new scenes вҖ” Rayleigh Haze (a warm sun reddening through a scattering atmosphere whose blue Пғ_t is ~6Г— its red, ringed by a blue single-scatter halo) and Amber Smoke (a plume coloured PURELY by its chromatic extinction over a neutral-grey albedo). Five new proofs (86 total): the per-wavelength homogeneous transmittance is EXACTLY e^(вҲ’Пғ_t(О»)L) (red transmits more than blue); ratio tracking through a constant field is unbiased at every О»; a chromatic pure-scattering furnace still returns EXACTLY 1 for ANY spectral Пғ_t (energy conservation, the unbiasedness oracle for the wavelength-resolved delta-tracking walk); a pure-absorbing haze reconstructs the SPECTRAL TRANSMITTANCE INTEGRAL (1/О”О»)вҲ«w(О»)В·e^(вҲ’Пғ_t(О»)В·2r)dО» matched to a fine quadrature and exits R>G>B; and an achromatic chromatic-medium collapses onto the scalar medium. Also (15.0) вҖ” SPECTRAL SUBSURFACE SCATTERING (A CHROMATIC MEAN FREE PATH): the reason a hand held over a torch glows deep red at the edges is NOT that red is absorbed less per bounce вҖ” it is that red light TRAVELS FURTHER inside the flesh before it is absorbed at all. In skin, marble and milk the EXTINCTION ITSELF is chromatic (red low, blue high), so red reaches the thin edges while blue scatters back out near the surface. Lumen's 12.0 subsurface walk had a SCALAR mean free path вҖ” one Пғ_t for every colour вҖ” so all the chromatic character had to come from the per-collision albedo; it could not express this. 15.0 adds it by reusing the HERO-WAVELENGTH machinery that already disperses glass and colours real metals: a path that refracts into a SPECTRAL interior commits to one wavelength О», takes its RGB weight ONCE (E_О»[w]=(1,1,1), so the estimator stays UNBIASED вҖ” colour is only a spread across О», never a change of mean), then random-walks MONOCHROMATICALLY with the extinction Пғ_t(О») and single-scattering albedo П–(О») resolved at that wavelength, the chromatic translucency reconstructing over many paths' wavelengths. Nothing in the transport loop changes except WHICH scalar Пғ_t/П– the interior free-flight uses, so the scalar 12.0 walk is kept bit-for-bit for any non-spectral interior and all 75 prior proofs stay green. The media are not hand-tuned: a new MEASURED BSSRDF LIBRARY carries the canonical coefficients of Jensen, Marschner, Levoy & Hanrahan (SIGGRAPH 2001, Table 2) вҖ” marble, skin, whole & skim milk, cream, ketchup, chicken, potato, apple вҖ” converting their reduced Пғ_sвҖІ and absorption Пғ_a into the per-wavelength extinction the walk consumes, so the new Living Skin (a measured-skin figurine raked from behind so its thin edges bleed red) and Apothecary (a shelf of spheres of real milk/marble/ketchup/cream, no two alike) scenes render REAL measured data. Six new proofs (81 total): the RGBвҶ’wavelength upsampling reproduces its control points and stays in envelope; every measured medium decodes to Пғ_t>0, П–вҲҲ[0,1] with red the deepest-penetrating for skin/marble; a chromatic pure-scatter furnace still returns EXACTLY 1 for ANY spectral Пғ_t (energy conservation, the unbiasedness oracle for the wavelength-resolved walk); a pure-absorbing slab reconstructs the SPECTRAL BEER INTEGRAL (1/О”О»)вҲ«w(О»)В·e^(вҲ’Пғ_t(О»)В·2r)dО» matched to a fine deterministic quadrature and exits R>G>B (chromatic penetration, rendered); a spectral interior with achromatic parameters collapses exactly onto the scalar 12.0 walk (the reduction oracle); and a measured skin slab glows red end-to-end. Also (14.0) вҖ” IMPORTANCE SAMPLING OF MANY LIGHTS (THE LIGHT BVH): next-event estimation has to choose WHICH emitter to connect a shadow ray to, and Lumen used to pick one UNIFORMLY вҖ” perfect for one ceiling light, catastrophic for hundreds, where almost every shadow ray is spent on a far, occluded or back-facing emitter and the floor renders as a storm of colour noise. 14.0 fixes the distribution (not the sample count) with the production-standard answer вҖ” a LIGHT BVH (Conty Estevez & Kulla 2018, the technique now in Arnold and PBRT-v4): a binary tree over the emissive triangles whose every node caches its TOTAL POWER, its BOUNDING BOX, and a BOUNDING CONE OF EMITTER NORMALS. To pick a light for a shade point p, the sampler walks the tree from the root, at each step choosing the child in proportion to a cheap, conservative estimate of how much that CLUSTER could light p вҖ” importance = powerВ·orientation(p)/distanceВІ(p,box) вҖ” accumulating the product of branch probabilities as the EXACT selection pdf, which the MIS weight reuses; so near, bright, well-oriented lights win the lottery and the shadow rays land where the light actually is. It is a drop-in for the SELECTION STEP ONLY: sampleLight/lightPdf keep their contract, so the NEEвҶ”BSDF MIS is untouched and the estimator stays UNBIASED вҖ” the tree only reshapes the variance of next-event estimation, never its mean, so a tree-sampled render converges to the SAME image a uniform one does, it just gets there far faster. Three guarantees make that airtight and PROVEN in the verify suite: the per-light selection probabilities SUM TO 1 at every p (a probability tree, normalised at each split); EVERY light keeps a strictly positive probability (a floored orientation term + clamped distance вҮ’ no contributing light is ever excluded, the precondition for unbiasedness); and the SAMPLER MATCHES THE PDF to Monte-Carlo precision (the same branch probabilities drive sample and prob). It reduces EXACTLY to the uniform sampler when the clusters carry equal importance (a coincident-lights oracle nails 1/N to machine Оө), and on a many-lights scene it cuts the NEE variance by HUNDREDS OF TIMES at equal mean. The uniform path is kept byte-for-byte as the default so every prior proof stays green; the tree is an opt-in toggle, on by default for two new showcase scenes вҖ” Star Field (a canopy of ~240 jewel-coloured emitters over a plaza) and Lantern Hall (a colonnade of warm lanterns where only the nearest few matter at any wall point). BDPT and the photon mapper sample lights their own way and are unaffected; the path tracer, the path-guided tracer and Metropolis all inherit the win. Five new proofs (75 total). Also (13.0) вҖ” PRACTICAL PATH GUIDING (THE SD-TREE): a FIFTH light-transport integrator that LEARNS its sampling distribution online (MГјller, Gross & NovГЎk 2017). As paths are traced, the radiance each carries is recorded into an SD-tree вҖ” a binary SPATIAL k-d tree over the scene whose every leaf owns a DIRECTIONAL quadtree over the sphere of directions, built on an EQUAL-AREA sphereвҶ”square map so its area measure is proportional to solid angle with a constant Jacobian (a flux-proportional quadtree is therefore a radiance-proportional sampler with no cosine warp to undo). Both trees refine between iterations (each renders twice the samples of the last): the spatial tree subdivides where many paths PASS (a visit counter, so it grows even before any light is found вҖ” from 2 leaves to hundreds) and the directional tree sharpens where a bright direction APPEARS. At each surface the next direction is drawn from the MIXTURE ОұВ·p_bsdf+(1вҲ’Оұ)В·p_guide and weighted by that exact density, with the next-event-estimation and emission MIS weights using the same mixture pdf so every estimator stays consistent. Because the guide is a genuine probability distribution over the sphere (its density INTEGRATES TO 1, proven in the verify suite) the estimator is UNBIASED for any learned distribution вҖ” guiding only reshapes the variance, never the mean, so Guided converges to the SAME image as the other four (proven by a GuidedвүЎpath-tracer oracle on the diffuse box). Its home turf is light next-event estimation cannot sample: the new Glowing Orb scene is lit only by an emissive SPHERE (Lumen's NEE samples only triangle lights, so it is invisible to it) вҖ” blind BSDF sampling finds it ~0.5% of the time and the plain path tracer is all fireflies, while the guide learns the orb's direction per region (~9% hit rate) and renders it with measurably lower error at equal samples. Three engineering choices make it a real win rather than a naive net-negative: per-leaf MATURITY GATING (an under-trained quadtree samples nearly uniformly, which is worse than cosine BSDF sampling, so a region only guides once mature, else falls back to BSDF while still recording), VISIT-DRIVEN spatial subdivision, and a gentle Оұ=0.7 (the piecewise-constant guide is a noisy sampler, so a 30% nudge atop 70% BSDF captures the win while bounding the variance, so guiding is never meaningfully worse than plain PT). Four new correctness proofs (DTree density вҲ«=1 over the sphere, samplerвҶ”pdf to machine Оө, ~100Г— variance cut on a peaked directional integral, and GuidedвүЎPT on the box). Also (12.0) вҖ” SUBSURFACE SCATTERING: a dielectric can now carry an interior SCATTERING medium (scalar extinction Пғ_t, per-channel single-scattering albedo, HenyeyвҖ“Greenstein g), turning glass into a TRANSLUCENT SOLID вҖ” marble, jade, wax, skin вҖ” that light refracts INTO, RANDOM-WALKS through and glows back OUT of. While the path is inside such a solid the integrator runs a homogeneous interior free-flight (distance вҲј e^(вҲ’Пғ_tВ·t)) bounded by the REAL surface geometry: a collision scatters off the phase function and multiplies throughput by the single-scattering albedo (so 1вҲ’albedo is absorbed at each step and the per-channel albedo IS the translucency's hue вҖ” why marble is warm-white and jade is green); reaching the boundary refracts the path out through the dielectric's exact Fresnel interface or TOTAL-INTERNALLY-REFLECTS it back in, trapping light to bounce again. It reuses the existing volume distance sampler, HG phase function and dielectric BSDF verbatim, so the transport loop is unchanged and all 61 prior proofs stay bit-for-bit green. The unmistakable subsurface glow a surface BRDF can never fake вҖ” the rim stays lit, the shadow side warms from within. Two showcase scenes (Subsurface Studio вҖ” a back-lit row of marble/jade/honey-wax/rose-quartz spheres; Jade Idol вҖ” a watertight hand-turned translucent lathe figurine) and FIVE correctness proofs: a pure-scattering index-matched furnace returns EXACTLY 1 for any phase g (zero variance вҖ” every path yields 1, proving the interior walk is unbiased and lossless), a pure-absorbing interior reproduces Beer's law e^(вҲ’ПғВ·2r) straight from the free-flight sampler, the WHOLE translucent object (Fresnel boundary + TIR + multiple internal scattering) conserves energy to вүҲ1, a per-channel albedo tints the exitance R>G>B (the marble/jade/skin mechanism), and the reflectance rises strictly monotonically with the interior albedo вҖ” all bounded вүӨ1. Also (11.0) вҖ” REAL METALS FROM MEASURED COMPLEX IOR: a conductor's colour is not an RGB tint but a spectral reflectance set by its complex refractive index nМ„(О»)=О·(О»)вҲ’ik(О»), so Lumen now carries measured О·/k spectra (gold/silver/copper from Johnson & Christy, aluminium from RakiДҮ, iron & chromium) and evaluates the EXACT unpolarised conductor Fresnel at the path's committed hero wavelength вҖ” the same machinery that disperses glass вҖ” so a metal's hue emerges spectrally: gold rims warm, copper reads red, silver stays bright-neutral and aluminium leans faintly blue, with the correct grazing desaturation that Schlick-from-RGB can never capture. The complex-IOR Fresnel rides on every existing lobe (single-scatter GGX, anisotropic brushed metal, AND the KullaвҖ“Conty multiscatter compensation) through one shared FresnelSpec, gated behind an optional spectrum field so the prior 55 proofs stay bit-for-bit green. New Metals of the World scene + six proofs вҖ” RвҲҲ[0,1] & grazingвҶ’1, the kвҶ’0 limit reducing EXACTLY to the dielectric Fresnel, the textbook metal colours, a furnace render reconstructing the measured RGB head-on, samplerвҶ”pdfвҶ”weight MIS consistency, and spectral multiscatter energy bounded by the hemispherical-average reflectance FМ„. Also (10.0) вҖ” a physically based MATERIAL SYSTEM that makes the surfaces as rigorous as the light transport: ENERGY-CONSERVING ROUGH METAL via a KullaвҖ“Conty multiple-scattering compensation lobe (driven by the GGX directional albedo E(Ој,Оұ) Monte-CarloвҖ“integrated into a table at start-up) so rough gold stops darkening to grey and stays bright and saturated вҖ” proven in-app to reflect вүҲ1 in a white furnace with compensation and measurably less without; ANISOTROPIC (brushed/milled) METAL with two GGX roughness axes in a rotatable tangent frame so the highlight stretches into a streak, reciprocity and energy preserved exactly; ORENвҖ“NAYAR rough-diffuse for chalk/clay/plaster/the-moon flat-matte look; and CLEAR-COAT layered materials (glazed ceramic, lacquer, car paint) вҖ” a GGX dielectric coat over a Lambert/OrenвҖ“Nayar base, the two lobes sampled by Fresnel weight with a combined pdf so the stack conserves energy AND stays MIS-consistent (the new BSDFs match BDPT pixel-for-pixel in the verify suite). Three new showcase scenes вҖ” Brushed Metal, Rough Conductors (a single-scatter vs multiscatter split), and Ceramics & Clay вҖ” plus six new correctness proofs. It carries FIVE selectable light-transport integrators: a unidirectional Monte-Carlo path tracer, the path-guided path tracer above (the learned SD-tree), a full bidirectional path tracer (BDPT) вҖ” VeachвҖ“Guibas cameraГ—light vertex connections weighted by balance-heuristic multiple importance sampling, which renders indirect-lit scenes (an emitter hidden in a cove) at a fraction of the noise вҖ” primary-sample-space Metropolis light transport (PSSMLT), a Markov chain run over the path tracer's own random-number stream (Kelemen mutation + Metropolis-Hastings acceptance, bootstrap normalisation, expected-value splatting, parallel chains across the worker pool) that locks onto the hardest-to-find light and refines the whole frame at once вҖ” the path tracer is reused verbatim as the contribution function because the Metropolis sampler simply IS the RNG вҖ” AND stochastic progressive photon mapping (SPPM), which runs the transport BACKWARDS to resolve the CAUSTICS the other three sample terribly (light focused through glass onto a floor вҖ” a lightвҶ’specularвҶ’diffuse path next-event estimation can't connect through the lens): photons are emitted from the area lights (power-sampled to equal flux), refracted through the specular geometry and deposited at per-pixel measurement points via an exact CSR spatial hash, while a jittered camera pass each iteration places those points and the per-point gather radius is shrunk on the Hachisuka schedule RВІвҶҗRВІВ·(N+ОұM)/(N+M) so the density-estimation bias provably vanishes and the estimate converges. Because all four estimators solve the same rendering equation, the in-app verification suite proves they converge to the SAME image (checking PSSMLT and SPPM against the path tracer on both global brightness and spatial distribution), that SPPM resolves a finite focused caustic the others miss, that the photon-gather grid returns exactly the in-radius points vs brute force, and that the MIS weights form an exact partition of unity. The photon mapper is also SPECTRAL вҖ” a photon commits a random hero wavelength on its first dispersive hit so the caustic it focuses through glass fans into a RAINBOW (proven to conserve total energy while spreading into colour) вҖ” and DAYLIGHT-complete: the sun is itself a photon emitter (photons leave a disc sized to the scene's bounding sphere as a parallel beam), so daylight scenes get photon-mapped SUN CAUSTICS, with the distant-light flux normalisation proven against the path tracer. Showcase scenes include a Caustic Pool вҖ” a sine-ripple water surface (analytic smooth normals) throwing the shifting bright filaments of a swimming pool. Plus participating media (fog/smoke/cloud) with Henyey-Greenstein scattering and volumetric god rays, thin-film interference for soap-bubble/oil-slick iridescence, smooth triangle meshes (icosphere, lathe & OBJ import) with barycentric-interpolated normals, a SAH bounding volume hierarchy, GGX microfacet BSDFs with VNDF sampling, smooth and frosted dielectrics, spectral dispersion (prism rainbows), Beer-Lambert volumetric absorption, procedural textures, an analytic Preetham daylight sky whose sun is a sampled light, next-event estimation with multiple importance sampling, scrambled-Halton low-discrepancy sampling, adaptive variance-guided sampling with a live noise heatmap, a multi-threaded Web Worker pool, ACES tone mapping, and an edge-avoiding ГҖ-Trous denoiser вҖ” global illumination on the CPU, in your browser. The participating media are now HETEROGENEOUS too: procedural 3D fBm density fields (cumulus clouds, rising smoke plumes, exponential ground-fog layers) traced unbiasedly with the null-collision estimators вҖ” DELTA TRACKING (Woodcock free-flight sampling against a constant majorant, accepting a real scatter with probability Пғ(x)/ПғМ„) for in-volume collisions and RATIO TRACKING for shadow-ray transmittance вҖ” so a cloud self-shadows into soft greys, flares a forward-scattering silver lining toward the sun, and breaks light into real volumetric beams, all from one continuously varying field, with five new proofs (delta tracking reproduces e^(вҲ’ПғL), ratio tracking matches a fine quadrature of a varying optical depth, and a heterogeneous pure-scattering volume conserves energy exactly). Media can also EMIT light volumetrically вҖ” at a real collision the path collects (1вҲ’albedo)В·LвӮ‘ of self-radiance, so a heterogeneous field glows brightest in its dense core (a soft, physically integrated fireball, not a billboard), proven to obey the emissionвҖ“absorption law (1вҲ’e^(вҲ’Пғ_tВ·chord))В·LвӮ‘; see the glowing Ember scene.
